title: Speedboat Rental in Estepona Port: Prices, Tips and Destinations for 2026
html:
Hiring a speedboat from Estepona Port gives you direct access to the Costa del Sol’s clearest waters, with half-day charters starting at around €350. Whether you want a quick coastal dash or a full day exploring hidden coves, BoatHire24 connects you with verified private owners and local operators who keep their craft in excellent condition.

Where to set off in Estepona Port

Most speedboat rentals depart from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, the modern marina at the western end of town. From there you can steer east toward the famous Marbella coastline or west to the quiet waters of the Genal River mouth. Popular stops include Playa del Cristo, the sheltered bay of Puerto de la Duquesa, and the offshore reefs near Cala Sardina. For a longer outing, head to the fishing village of Sotogrande or cross the bay to Gibraltar, roughly 16 nautical miles south. Many skippers also recommend anchoring at the Playa de la Rada for a swim before the afternoon breeze picks up.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat, best for couples or small families; up to four guests; from €250 for half a day.
  2. Sports speedboat, ideal for a coastal blast or water-skiing; up to six guests; from €400 for a half-day charter.
  3. RIB, a stable, fast option for exploring nearby coves; up to eight guests; from €350 for half a day.
  4. Crewed speedboat, a skippered experience with refreshments; up to ten guests; from €600 for a full day.
  5. Premium sports cruiser, models such as the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, 150 HP combine comfort with performance; up to seven guests; from €500 for a half day. You can check availability and see full specifications on this speedboat listing.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Estepona Port?

Prices for a half-day rental range from €250 for a small motorboat to over €900 for a luxury speedboat with a crew. The final cost depends on the boat’s size, age, and whether you hire a skipper. Typical price bands are as follows:

  • Shared tour (2, 3 hours), from €80 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours), €250, €400.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests), €700, €1,200.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (skippered, with fuel and insurance), €900, €1,500.

BoatHire24 lists exactly what is included, so you can compare deals without any hidden extras. To see verified options, rent a boat in Estepona Port directly from owners or local charter companies.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Estepona Port?

In Spain, the licence requirement depends on the boat’s engine power and length; for most rental speedboats, no licence is needed if you take a skipper. Spanish law requires a Recreational Navigation Licence (Licencia de Navegación) for boats with engines over 15 kW (about 20 hp) if you want to drive yourself. However, nearly all rental speedboats in Estepona Port exceed that power, so operators either include a qualified skipper or ask to see an equivalent international permit. The exact requirements vary by boat, so the operator will confirm what documents you need when you book.

Tip: book a morning departure in high season (June to September) to avoid the levante wind, which often picks up after 2 p.m. and makes the sea choppy along the exposed coast.

At a glance

  • Price range: €250, €1,500 per half/full day.
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September.
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours).
  • Maximum guest capacity on standard speedboats: 7, 10 persons.
  • Currency accepted: EUR.

Questions about speedboat rental in Estepona Port

Can I rent a speedboat without a skipper?

You can, provided you hold a valid Spanish Recreational Navigation Licence or an equivalent international certificate, though most rental boats in Estepona Port come with a skipper included.

What is the best time of year to hire a speedboat in Estepona?

The best time is from May to October, when the sea is calm and air temperatures range from 22°C to 30°C, but July and August are the busiest months.

How far can I travel in a half-day rental?

A half-day trip (four hours) typically allows you to reach Puerto de la Duquesa, about 5 nautical miles west, or Marbella, about 8 nautical miles east, with time for a swim stop.

Is fuel included in the rental price?

Fuel is sometimes included in full-day charters but is often charged as an extra for half-day rentals; check the listing details before booking.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Estepona Port or list your own. To start planning your 2026 adventure, browse the listings and compare boats, prices, and availability. If you own a boat in Estepona Port, you can list it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of potential renters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona Port and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
